text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. Refactoring does not fix bugs or add new features; instead, it focuses on reducing technical debt—the accumulated issues that make future changes slow and risky. Common refactoring actions include renaming unclear variables, breaking long functions into smaller ones, and removing duplicated logic. By regularly refactoring, developers keep the codebase flexible and understandable, which saves time and effort in the long run.
